FilesOrScope: One focused system/file set selected by executive request
PlainEnglishQuestion: Do you want to name the next narrow, non-gated source improvement for SOURCE-BATCH-004?
PlainEnglishContext: The audit controller has completed SOURCE-BATCH-001 through SOURCE-BATCH-003. There is no hidden queue of approved source-safe work left. To keep improving code without crossing policy gates, you need to name one concrete behavior, system, and expected file set.
WhatApprovalAllows: A future goal can implement one focused non-gated repair or feature, after proving it does not cross the POST-BATCH-Y fences.
WhatStaysBlockedIfNotApproved: No additional non-gated source batch opens; gated staff, balance, region, serializer, and reorganization work remain blocked.
RecommendedDefault: Leave blank until you have a concrete behavior you want changed.
AnswerOptions: Name next target | Leave pending | Ask for candidate discovery list
ExecutiveDecisionDetails: Find candidate non-gated source improvements from the audit/source tree. Prefer small safety/guard/bug-fix work. Do not cross staff, balance, region, serializer, project/config/data, or reorganization gates.
RiskIfApproved: Low to medium if the target is narrow; risk depends on touched system and whether runtime hooks or serializers are involved.
RiskIfDeferred: No new source improvements happen beyond already committed batches.
AutomationSuitability: Good after target is concrete; one behavior per goal.
CanRunAsOneGoal: Yes, for one concrete behavior/system/file set only.
RequiredApprovalSpecifics: Provide behavior to change, system, expected files if known, and what must stay unchanged.
VerificationNeeded: POST-BATCH-Y gate preflight; targeted source scan; serializer/hook/command scan if touched; Server.csproj Debug/x86 build; compile-only runtime verification; git diff --check.
SourceAuditEvidence: docs/codebase-audit/outputs/source-batch-controller-roadmap-status.csv; docs/codebase-audit/outputs/source-batch-controller-closeout.md
NotesForCoder: Do not invent the target. If this row is unanswered, keep SOURCE-BATCH-004+ pending.

FilesOrScope: All approved decision rows after executive fills this CSV
PlainEnglishQuestion: After you fill in decisions, may approved rows be processed by an automated sequential goal runner, or should each decision become its own goal?
PlainEnglishContext: A sequential runner can save time by processing multiple approved rows in order, but grouped source changes increase review and rollback complexity. One-goal-per-decision is slower but safer for policy, serializer, and reorganization work.
WhatApprovalAllows: Defines how future approved rows from this CSV may be converted into implementation goals.
WhatStaysBlockedIfNotApproved: Future agents should default to one goal per approved decision or ask before grouping.
RecommendedDefault: One goal per approved decision for gated work; sequential runner only for low-risk non-gated repairs.
AnswerOptions: One goal per decision | Sequential runner for approved rows | Sequential runner only for non-gated rows | Ask before each batch
RiskIfApproved: A broad runner may mix unrelated risks, making review and rollback harder.
RiskIfDeferred: More manual goal writing and slower throughput, but better control.
AutomationSuitability: This row defines automation policy.
CanRunAsOneGoal: Yes, this is a governance row only.
RequiredApprovalSpecifics: State which categories can be grouped, max rows per goal, and whether commits must happen after each row or each batch.
VerificationNeeded: Every implementation still needs category-specific verification from the matched gate rows.
SourceAuditEvidence: docs/codebase-audit/outputs/post-batch-aa-source-batch-roadmap.csv; docs/codebase-audit/outputs/source-batch-controller-roadmap-status.csv
NotesForCoder: If unanswered, do not run a broad automation goal across gated categories.

SystemOrRelationship: All folder and namespace cleanup rows
FilesOrScope: PvP Consent; Invasion; XMLSpawner; Government; Offline Skill Training; Homestead
PlainEnglishQuestion: For reorganization, do you want file moves only, namespace/type renames, or no reorganization yet?
PlainEnglishContext: File moves can improve layout while keeping saved object identity stable. Namespace/type renames are much riskier because old RunUO saves bind objects to namespace and type names. Reorg also requires docs, project hygiene, runtime compile visibility, and rollback evidence.
WhatApprovalAllows: Sets the default policy for future reorganization rows before system-specific move plans are written.
WhatStaysBlockedIfNotApproved: Folder/package cleanup remains blocked for the six listed systems.
RecommendedDefault: Approve file moves only when there is a specific plan; no namespace/type renames by default; one system at a time.
AnswerOptions: File moves only | Namespace/type renames allowed with migration plan | Defer all reorganization | Request detailed move plan first
RiskIfApproved: Moves can break project references, docs source traces, runtime compile visibility, or save identity if the scope is too broad.
RiskIfDeferred: Code remains harder to navigate, but runtime and save compatibility risk stays low.
AutomationSuitability: Limited. Create one reorganization goal per system after this global policy is answered.
CanRunAsOneGoal: No for all systems together; yes for one approved system move plan.
RequiredApprovalSpecifics: State allowed move type, whether namespaces/types may change, target layout preference, rollback requirement, and whether Scripts.csproj IDE hygiene must be maintained.
VerificationNeeded: Project truth; runtime compile visibility; serialization register; dependency graph; docs source traces; Scripts.csproj if needed; Server.csproj Debug/x86 build; compile-only runtime verification; rollback check; git diff --check.
SourceAuditEvidence: docs/codebase-audit/outputs/post-batch-y-source-change-gate-register.csv rows with Category=Folder and namespace cleanup
NotesForCoder: Even with global approval, still require a per-system move plan before editing.

FilesOrScope: HouseFoundation and any future serialized type touched by reorganization or serializer work
PlainEnglishQuestion: Should the project preserve serialized namespace/type identity by default, with migrations only by explicit approval?
PlainEnglishContext: RunUO world saves depend on serialized type names and exact read/write order. This policy protects player/world data. It means cleanup work should avoid renaming serialized types or changing save order unless a migration plan is explicitly approved.
WhatApprovalAllows: Establishes a default safety rule for future source changes and reorganization: preserve save identity unless a row-specific migration is approved.
WhatStaysBlockedIfNotApproved: Agents must continue treating serializer renames/order changes as blocked and ask before touching them.
RecommendedDefault: Yes: preserve serialized identity and save order by default; require explicit migration approval for any exception.
AnswerOptions: Preserve by default | Allow migrations with explicit plan | Defer serializer policy decision
RiskIfApproved: Preserve-by-default has low runtime risk but limits cleanup freedom. Migration approval carries high save compatibility risk.
RiskIfDeferred: Future serializer work remains blocked or repeatedly asks for approval.
AutomationSuitability: Good as a global guardrail; actual serializer changes remain standalone goals.
SuggestedGoalShape: Global policy row plus standalone GATED-SOURCE-BATCH-HOUSEFOUNDATION if migration is approved.
CanRunAsOneGoal: Yes for policy recording only; no for serializer implementation.
RequiredApprovalSpecifics: State default serializer policy and whether any exceptions are allowed without separate approval.
VerificationNeeded: For implementation exceptions: before/after serializer map, old-save compatibility proof, Server.csproj Debug/x86 build, compile-only runtime verification, save-load test where available, git diff --check.
SourceAuditEvidence: docs/codebase-audit/outputs/post-batch-y-source-change-gate-register.csv row RB-00724; serialization-register.csv
NotesForCoder: If unanswered, preserve serialized identity and do not change serializer order.

FilesOrScope: Relationship-level row; no single source file is approved for policy edits from this row.
PlainEnglishQuestion: Do you approve tuning the reward, progression, economy, or gameplay balance relationship for Character Level <-> Offline Skill Training?
PlainEnglishContext: This is not classified as a code defect. It is a gameplay design choice about how fast players progress, what rewards are worth, and how two systems reinforce or bypass each other.
WhatApprovalAllows: A focused source batch may change reward rates, progression incentives, economy loops, spawn rewards, vendor behavior, crafting/harvest/gardening loops, or paired-system tuning for this relationship.
WhatStaysBlockedIfNotApproved: Current gameplay balance stays preserved for this relationship; code correctness fixes outside tuning may still proceed.
RecommendedDefault: Preserve current tuning unless you have a specific desired gameplay outcome.
AnswerOptions: Approve tuning with stated outcome | Preserve current tuning | Request design proposal | Reject tuning
RiskIfApproved: Can affect player economy, progression speed, farming value, event rewards, or perceived fairness.
RiskIfDeferred: Known balance concerns remain, but player-facing economy and progression stay stable.
AutomationSuitability: Partly automatable after a design answer; implementation should be one relationship or reward path per goal.
CanRunAsOneGoal: No, unless multiple rows are intentionally approved under one balance design document.
RequiredApprovalSpecifics: State desired tuning policy, target system/relationship, what should increase/decrease, and acceptance notes.
VerificationNeeded: Source review of reward/economy paths; affected docs update if behavior changes; gameplay acceptance notes; Server.csproj Debug/x86 build; compile-only runtime verification; git diff --check.

PlainEnglishQuestion: Do you approve reorganizing files or packages for PvP Consent?

WhatApprovalAllows: A focused reorganization batch may move files or packages for this system within the approved constraints.
WhatStaysBlockedIfNotApproved: Folder moves, namespace changes, type renames, project path rewrites, package containment moves, serialized type relocation, and docs move updates for this system remain blocked.
RecommendedDefault: Approve file moves only when needed, avoid namespace/type renames, one system at a time, with rollback.
AnswerOptions: Approve file moves only | Approve namespace/type changes with migration plan | Defer reorganization | Request detailed move plan
RiskIfApproved: Can break runtime compile visibility, IDE project hygiene, docs source traces, or old save compatibility if not tightly verified.
RiskIfDeferred: Code remains less organized, but save and runtime identity risk stays low.
AutomationSuitability: Automatable only after a specific move list and rollback policy; one system per goal.
CanRunAsOneGoal: No. Reorganization should be one system per goal.
RequiredApprovalSpecifics: Name current paths, target paths, whether namespaces/types may change, save compatibility expectations, docs/project updates, and rollback plan.
